metadata.name: Scoring Rubric
metadata.description: Quantifies policy dimensions (authority, veto, logging, escalation) into scoreable criteria.
metadata.use_case: Score any system's process output against the same evaluation frame, regardless of topology.

dimensions.name: Authority Compliance
dimensions.description: Verifies that the decision-maker had requisite authority to execute the action.

criteria.label: Full Compliance
criteria.definition: Authority verified, logged, and enforced before execution.

criteria.label: Partial Compliance
criteria.definition: Authority verified post-hoc; logged but not enforced pre-execution.

criteria.label: Minimal Compliance
criteria.definition: Authority claimed but not independently verified; logged after fact.

criteria.label: No Compliance
criteria.definition: No authority check; execution proceeded without verification.

dimensions.name: Veto Integrity
dimensions.description: Ensures that vetoes are respected and documented when encountered.

criteria.label: Full Integrity
criteria.definition: Veto detected, execution halted, veto reason logged, escalation path initiated.

criteria.label: Partial Integrity
criteria.definition: Veto detected and halted, but reason logging incomplete or escalation delayed.

criteria.label: No Integrity
criteria.definition: Veto ignored or undetected; execution proceeded despite veto signal.

dimensions.name: Logging Completeness
dimensions.description: Measures the breadth and precision of process events recorded.

criteria.label: Full Completeness
criteria.definition: All decision points, authority checks, vetoes, escalations, and outcomes logged with timestamps and context.

criteria.label: Partial Completeness
criteria.definition: Major decision points logged; minor events or context omitted.

criteria.label: Minimal Completeness
criteria.definition: Only outcome logged; decision chain incomplete or missing timestamps.

criteria.label: No Completeness
criteria.definition: No meaningful logging; audit trail absent or unintelligible.

dimensions.name: Escalation Correctness
dimensions.description: Verifies that escalation rules are applied correctly when thresholds or conditions are met.

criteria.label: Full Correctness
criteria.definition: Escalation triggered at correct threshold; escalated to correct authority; documented with reason and context.

criteria.label: Partial Correctness
criteria.definition: Escalation triggered but escalated to suboptimal authority; reason documented but incomplete.

criteria.label: Minimal Correctness
criteria.definition: Escalation triggered late or to wrong authority; minimal documentation.

criteria.label: No Correctness
criteria.definition: Escalation not triggered despite meeting threshold; no documentation.

notes: Process score represents the degree to which a system's decision-making adheres to policy-defined governance controls. A score of 1.0 indicates complete compliance across all dimensions; 0.0 indicates systemic failures in governance.
